title: Generate Python lifecycle root commands

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Migrate root Python lifecycle commands install, init, upgrade, and uninstall into the generated command package while preserving hand-owned lifecycle runtime behavior.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Stay on the current branch. Do not create a PR. Do not migrate prompt, external-intent, system-intent, or note-delegation-outcome in this slice. Generated code owns parser/help/dispatch; runtime lifecycle mutation policy stays hand-owned behind thin adapters.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Command-package IR entries, lifecycle runtime adapter binding names, generated artifacts, and focused conformance/static proof for lifecycle root commands.
canonical_core.escalate_when: Implementation requires changing lifecycle semantics, weakening preflight/destructive safeguards, or broadening into prompt/external/system intent command surfaces.
canonical_core.next_action: Add generated command-package entries for install, init, upgrade, and uninstall; bind thin lifecycle runtime adapters; regenerate command package artifacts; and prove lifecycle dry run plus refusal conformance remains intact.
proof_expectations: uv run pytest tests/test_contract_tooling.py tests/test_generated_command_package_proof_runner.py tests/test_workspace_packaging.py tests/test_workspace_cli_blackbox.py -q
completion_criteria: agentic-workspace --help lists install/init/upgrade/uninstall from generated root command metadata.
install/init/upgrade/uninstall --help parse through generated command-package support.
Lifecycle dry-run and refusal conformance remain intact.
Handwritten root parser no longer owns the migrated lifecycle command shapes.
Prompt, external-intent, system-intent, and note-delegation-outcome remain explicit continuation.
